---
title: "prepare data on the Gribben et. al dataset"
output:
  html_document: default
  word_document: default
  pdf_document: default
date: "June 2026"
---


```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(Seurat)
library(SeuratWrappers)
library(dplyr)
#library(gprofiler2)
library(ggplot2)
#library(ggrepel)
library(SeuratDisk)
#library(anndataR)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(scCustomize) # Used for robust, seamless .h5ad export
set.seed(42)

```


Prepare seurat object for Python reading
Let’s begin by loading a pre-processed Seurat object, which is ready for clustering
 (the adjacency matrix has already been computed using Seurat’s FindNeighbors) and visualise the cell types/annotations.
 
```{r load_object, cache=FALSE}

# download data from https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE202379

full <- readRDS("C:/Users/Floarea/Desktop/corebioinfo/codes/data_processing/input_data/GSE202379_SeuratObject_AllCells.rds")

# meta <- full@meta.data %>%
#   mutate(Disease.status = factor(Disease.status, levels = disease_order))

# # Hepatocyte/Cholangiocyte composition within each disease

# meta %>%
#   filter(cell.annotation %in% c("Hepatocytes", "Cholangiocytes")) %>%
#   count(Disease.status, cell.annotation) %>%
#   group_by(Disease.status) %>%
#   mutate(percent = round(100 * n / sum(n), 2))

# # Disease composition of entire dataset

# meta %>%
#   count(Disease.status) %>%
#   mutate(percent = round(100 * n / sum(n), 2))


#DefaultAssay(full)
#dim(full[["RNA"]]) #31257 99809
#dim(full[["SCT"]]) #30117 99809

# # keep only the cells we're interested in
cells_keep <- colnames(full)[
    full$cell.annotation %in% c("Hepatocytes", "Cholangiocytes") &
    full$Disease.status == "end stage"]

#length(cells_keep) #25527
seurat_object <- subset(full,cells = cells_keep)
rm(full)

# # Plot the UMAP reduction to visualize cell annotations
DimPlot(seurat_object, reduction="umap_harmony_t.0", group.by = "cell.annotation")

```


Remove the bottom island to focus on the population with the hepatocyte-cholangocyte bridge.

```{r remove_island}
# # Remove bottom island - all below -6 on UMAP_2
umap_df <- as.data.frame(seurat_object@reductions$umap_harmony_t.0@cell.embeddings)
colnames(umap_df) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")
cell_names_keep <- rownames(umap_df[umap_df$UMAP_1 > 4 & umap_df$UMAP_2 > -10, ])
seurat_object <- subset(seurat_object, cells = cell_names_keep)
DimPlot(seurat_object, reduction="umap_harmony_t.0", group.by = "cell.annotation")

# # FIX: Strip corrupted SCTModel slots - This is required to bypass the 'containsOutOfMemoryData' error
# if ("SCT" %in% names(seurat_object@assays)) {
#     message("Cleaning SCTModel slots to prevent saving errors...")
#     seurat_object[["SCT"]]@SCTModel.list <- list()
# }
# 
# # Define save path
# input_dir <- "C:/Users/Floarea/Desktop/corebioinfo/codes/data_processing/input_data"
# rds_output <- file.path(input_dir, "hep_chol_endstate_truncated.rds")
# 
# # Save the object
# message("Saving object to: ", rds_output)
# saveRDS(seurat_object, file = rds_output, compress = TRUE)
```



```{r keep only protein coding genes}

# # Load the Master Seurat Object ──────────────────────────────────────────
# input_dir   <- "C:/Users/Floarea/Desktop/corebioinfo/codes/data_processing/input_data"
# seurat_path <- file.path(input_dir, "hep_chol_endstate_truncated.rds")
# seurat_object <- readRDS(seurat_path)

#seurat_object <- readRDS(seurat_path)
print(paste("Original Dataset:", nrow(seurat_object), "genes ×", ncol(seurat_object), "cells"))

# Identify Protein-Coding Genes ──────────────────────────────────────────
all_genes <- rownames(seurat_object)

# Query the database for gene types
gene_mapping <- select(org.Hs.eg.db,
                       keys = all_genes,
                       columns = c("SYMBOL", "GENETYPE"),
                       keytype = "SYMBOL")

# Extract only genes explicitly flagged as "protein-coding"
protein_coding_genes <- gene_mapping$SYMBOL[gene_mapping$GENETYPE == "protein-coding"]
protein_coding_genes <- protein_coding_genes[!is.na(protein_coding_genes)]

# Filter the Seurat Object ───────────────────────────────────────────────
full_protein_coding <- subset(seurat_object, features = protein_coding_genes)
print(paste("Filtered Dataset:", nrow(full_protein_coding), "genes ×", ncol(full_protein_coding), "cells"))

# # OUTPUT 1: Save as R Data Structure (.rds) ──────────────────────────────
# rds_output <- file.path(input_dir, "hep_chol_endstate_truncated_protein_coding.rds")
# saveRDS(full_protein_coding, file = rds_output)
# print(paste("📂 [1/2] R-ready file saved to:", rds_output))

# # OUTPUT 2: Save as Python Data Structure (.h5ad) ────────────────────────
# scCustomize handles the conversion of internal metadata (obs) and counts (X)
# smoothly without breaking sparse matrix indexing.
print("Converting and writing .h5ad file for Python...")
as.anndata(
  x = full_protein_coding,
  file_path = input_dir,
  file_name = "hep_chol_endstate_truncated_protein_coding.h5ad",
  assay = "RNA" # Speeds up export by saving your standard raw/log count matrices
)



```
